response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile software engineer with a strong web/full-stack background, but he does not meet the role's hard requirements. There is no indication of any Japanese proficiency (the role requires native-level Japanese), no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 switching, embedded networking, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X. His C/C++ experience is brief and dated (a ~7-month stint in 2014-2015), while his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang web systems, making him a poor fit for embedded firmware security development on LAN switches.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some hardware-adjacent projects
Some professional C/C++ experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and low-latency Linux systems work in DLang
Strong engineering culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No Japanese language ability listed; native-level Japanese is a required qualification
No experience with Linux kernel modification, L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X, captive portal)
C programming experience is minimal and dated; recent work is almost entirely web/full-stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking, Linux kernel, or security-protocol experience
Solid general engineering experience with some C/C++ and Linux exposure
